sarga.title: Lakshmana gets pacified -- Sugriva praises Rama's valour -- Lakshmana tenders apology for his harsh words

shlokas.original:
इत्युक्तस्तारया वाक्यं प्रश्रितं धर्मसंहितम्।
मृदुस्वभावस्सौमित्रिः प्रतिजग्राह तद्वचः।।4.36.1।।
shlokas.translation: Thus the gentlenatured Saumitri accepted Tara's courteous words sanctioned by dharma.

shlokas.original:
तस्मिन्प्रतिगृहीते तु वाक्ये हरिगणेश्वरः।
लक्ष्मणात्सुमहत्त्रासं वस्त्रं क्लिन्नमिवात्यजत्।।4.36.2।।
shlokas.translation: Sugriva, king of the vanara clan, cast aside his great fear when Lakshmana accepted the words of Tara just as one discards a wet garment from the body.

shlokas.original:
ततः कण्ठगतं माल्यं चित्रं बहुगुणं महत्।
चिच्छेद विमदश्चासीत्सुग्रीवो वानरेश्वरः।।4.36.3।।
shlokas.translation: Thereupon Sugriva, relieved from his drunken state tore off the great, colourful valuable garland worn on his neck.

shlokas.original:
स लक्ष्मणं भीमबलं सर्ववानरसत्तमः।
अब्रवीत्प्रश्रितं वाक्यं सुग्रीव स्सम्प्रहर्षयन्।।4.36.4।।
shlokas.translation: Sugriva, the noblest of all monkeys, spoke these polite words in a pleasing manner to Lakshmana of terrific strength:

shlokas.original:
प्रणष्टा श्रीश्च कीर्तिश्च कपिराज्यं च शाश्वतम्।
रामप्रसादात्सौमित्रे पुन: प्राप्तमिदं मया।।4.36.5।।
shlokas.translation: 'O Saumitri my prosperity, fame and the permanent sovereignty of vanaras had been lost. It was  regained by the grace of Rama.

shlokas.original:
कश्शक्तस्तस्य देवस्य ख्यातस्य स्वेन कर्मणा।
तादृशं विक्रमं वीर प्रतिकर्तुमरिन्दम।।4.36.6।।
shlokas.translation: 'O crusher of enemies  how is it possible to repay the famous lord Rama's valour (in  killing Vali)?

shlokas.original:
सीतां प्राप्स्यति धर्मात्मा वधिष्यति च रावणम्।
सहायमात्रेण मया राघव स्स्वेन तेजसा।।4.36.7।।
shlokas.translation: 'Righteous Rama can merely restore Sita with my assistance. By his own fiery energy he will kill Ravana.

shlokas.original:
सहायकृत्यं किं तस्य येन सप्त महाद्रुमाः।
शैलश्च वसुधा चैव बाणेनैकेन दारिताः।।4.36.8।।
shlokas.translation: 'Rama shattered seven huge trees, a mountain and earth with a single arrow. What assistance does he need?

shlokas.original:
धनुर्विष्फारयानस्य यस्य शब्देन लक्ष्मण।
सशैला कम्पिता भूमिस्सहायै: किन्नु तस्य वै।।4.36.9।।
shlokas.translation: 'O Lakshmana the twang of his bow shook even the mountains and the earth. What assistance does he need?

shlokas.original:
अनुयात्रां नरेन्द्रस्य करिष्येऽहं नरर्षभ।
गच्छतो रावणं हन्तुं वैरिणं सपुरस्सरम्।।4.36.10।।
shlokas.translation: 'O Lakshmana, bull among men I shall only join him along with others when he leads the army to kill Ravana.

shlokas.original:
यदि किञ्चिदतिक्रान्तं विश्वासात्प्रणयेन वा।
प्रेष्यस्य क्षमितव्यं मे न कश्चिन्नापराध्यति।।4.36.11।।
shlokas.translation: 'If I have committed even a little transgression in my faith in Rama or affection for him and exceeded the time limit, my mistakes are to be excused as one would pardons a servant. No one is flawless in the world.'

shlokas.original:
ति तस्य ब्रुवाणस्य सुग्रीवस्य महात्मनः।
अभवल्लक्ष्मणः प्रीतः प्रेम्णा चैवमुवाच ह।।4.36.12।।
shlokas.translation: Lakshmana who was happy to hear great Sugriva replied affectionately:

shlokas.original:
सर्वथा हि मम भ्राता सनाथो वानरेश्वर।
त्वया नाथेन सुग्रीव प्रश्रितेन विशेषतः।।4.36.13।।
shlokas.translation: 'O Sugriva, king of monkeys when you are extending due courtesy to my brother,he has a guardian in you by all means.

shlokas.original:
यस्ते प्रभाव स्सुग्रीव यच्च ते शौचमार्जवम्।
अर्हस्त्वं कपिराज्यस्य श्रियं भोक्तुमनुत्तमाम्।।4.36.14।।
shlokas.translation: 'You are powerful and straightforward by nature.You are humble and noble. Therefore, you deserve the high prosperity of the kingdom of the monkeys.

shlokas.original:
सहायेन तु सुग्रीव त्वया रामः प्रतापवान्।
वधिष्यति रणे शत्रूनचिरान्नात्र संशयः।।4.36.15।।
shlokas.translation: 'O Sugriva with you as associate, valiant hero Rama will kill his enemies in war very soon. There is no doubt about it.

shlokas.original:
धर्मज्ञस्य कृतज्ञस्य सङ्ग्रामेष्वनिवर्तिनः।
उपपन्नं च युक्तं च सुग्रीव तव भाषितम्।।4.36.16।।
shlokas.translation: 'O Sugriva you know dharma.You are grateful and will not turn back from the battle. So your words are true and befitting.

shlokas.original:
दोषज्ञस्सति सामर्थ्ये कोऽन्यो भाषितुमर्हति।
वर्जयित्वा मम ज्येष्ठं त्वां च वानरसत्तम।।4.36.17।।
shlokas.translation: 'O best of monkeys barring you and my elder brother, who else would be capable of speaking this way about themselves even when you are aware of other's faults?

shlokas.original:
सदृशश्चासि रामस्य विक्रमेण बलेन च।
सहायो दैवतैर्दत्तश्चिराय हरिपुङ्गव।।4.36.18।।
shlokas.translation: 'You who are equal to Rama in valour and strength, will continue to be the gift of gods to us for long.

shlokas.original:
किं तु शीघ्रमितो वीर निष्क्रम त्वं मया सह।
सान्त्वयस्व वयस्यं च त्वं भार्याहरणकर्शितम्।।4.36.19।।
shlokas.translation: 'Nevertheless I want you to proceed from here with me at once and reassure your friend who is agrieved due to the abduction of his wife.

shlokas.original:
यच्च शोकाभिभूतस्य श्रुत्वा रामस्य भाषितम्।
मया त्वं परुषाण्युक्तस्तच्चत्वं क्षन्तु मर्हसि।।4.36.20।।
shlokas.translation: इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामयणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये किष्किन्धाकाण्डे षट्त्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtysix sarga of Kishkindakanda of the Holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
